title: Writing - Part 1: Family and Relationships
description: Your class has been discussing how family relationships have changed in modern society.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the changes in your notes. You should explain which change you think has had the greatest impact on family life, giving reasons for your opinion.

changes: The influence of technology on family time
opinions: Many families today barely spend time together due to busy schedules.
Technology can help families stay connected across distances.
Children benefit from having two working parents as role models.

structure.introduction: Introduce changes in modern family life and the two changes you will discuss.
structure.bodyParagraph1: Discuss the first change (e.g., both parents working) and its effects on family relationships.
structure.bodyParagraph2: Discuss the second change (e.g., technology's influence on family time) and how it affects relationships.
structure.conclusion: Evaluate both changes and state which has had the greatest impact on family life.

content.modelAnswer:
The nature of family life has changed considerably over the past few decades. Economic pressures, greater mobility and the rise of digital technology have all transformed how families interact. This essay will discuss the impact of dual working households and the increasing role of technology in family life.

The rise of families where both parents work full-time has had a significant effect on home life. While this arrangement is often financially necessary and provides children with positive role models, it can reduce the time available for shared meals, conversations and activities. Children who spend less quality time with their parents may feel less supported and connected to their families.

Technology has further complicated family relationships. While apps and video calls allow relatives who live apart to stay in touch, screens can also become a barrier within the same household. Many families find themselves sitting together but engaged with separate devices, reducing opportunities for genuine face-to-face communication.

In conclusion, I believe technology's influence on family time is the more significant change. The physical presence of parents at home has always been valuable, but when people are together yet still distracted by devices, the quality of family relationships suffers greatly. Learning to use technology wisely within the family home is an important challenge for modern society.
